medical_history.chief_complaint: Acute-onset consciousness disturbance, fever, and elevated serum creatinine kinase levels
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Acute-onset consciousness disturbance (a Japanese coma scale score of 200), fever, and elevated serum creatinine kinase levels in mid-September 2010. Convulsion was noted in late-September. Treatment with intravenous acyclovir and methylprednisolone pulse therapy was partially effective. The patient continued to have dementia with myoclonus and psychiatric symptoms despite resolution of the consciousness disturbance.
medical_history.past_medical_history: No medical or familial history of central nervous system disease

physical_examination.general_examination: Disturbed consciousness gradually improved, and myoclonus was observed in his right upper limb. Dementia, psychiatric symptoms, a startle reaction, myoclonus, truncal and limb rigidity, and spasticity in all four limbs were noted. Muscle weakness, atrophy, and fasciculation were not observed.
physical_examination.specialty_examination: Neurological examination showed dementia, psychiatric symptoms, a startle reaction, myoclonus, truncal and limb rigidity, and spasticity in all four limbs. Periodic sharp-wave complexes were not observed on the serial electroencephalograms.

laboratory_examinations.findings: Normal cell count, mildly elevated protein concentration (59 mg/dl)
40 ng/ml during acute phase, 11 ng/ml after convulsions were controlled
Prion protein in the CSF was slightly and slowly amplified
Methionine homozygosity at codon 129, no mutations

radiographic_examinations.findings: No obvious finding initially; later revealed cortical edematous hyper-intense areas in the bilateral frontal, insula, central posterior, and cingulate gyri
radiographic_examinations.changes_over_time: Hyper-intense signals diminished within 1 month; progressive atrophy of the brain cortices, mainly the temporal lobes

other_tests.findings: Focal brain softening, severe atrophy with loss of neuronal cells, gliosis, and macrophages in hippocampal CA1 to subiculum; laminar necrosis in the second and third cortical layers in cingulate gyrus and frontal cortex; mild neuronal loss and gliosis in deep cortical layers; mild loss of Purkinje cells; no PrP deposits

other_tests.procedure_name: Western blot analysis of protease-resistant PrP

diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on steroid responsiveness, absence of well-characterized onconeuronal antibodies or autoimmune encephalitis associated autoantibodies, and the clinical course. The positive RT-QUIC result initially suggested prion disease, but postmortem evaluation excluded prion disease based on PrP immunostaining and Western blot results. The pathological changes were attributed to convulsions.

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: 1500 mg/day until CSF tested negative for HSV genomic PCR
